evals.name: messy-commits-cleanup
evals.prompt: hey so i've been working on this branch (DE-4521/add-user-engagement-model) for the past 2 days and I think it's finally ready. I've got like 8 commits but some of them are just 'fix tests' and 'wip' stuff. Can you help me get this ready for review? The main changes are a new dbt model called user_engagement that aggregates firebase events.
evals.expected_output: Should validate commits, suggest squashing the messy ones, generate proper PR title like '(DE-4521) feat(dbt): add user engagement model', create PR with description, and generate #data-review message

assertions.name: PR title matches regex format
assertions.description: The generated PR title must match the required format: ^(\((DA-[0-9]+|DE-[0-9]+|HF-[0-9]+|BSR|)\) )?(build|lint|ci|docs|feat|fix|perf|refactor|test|core|dbt|)\(\w+\): \w+

assertions.name: PR title includes correct ticket ID
assertions.description: The PR title should include (DE-4521) since that's the ticket mentioned in the branch name

assertions.name: PR title uses 'dbt' scope
assertions.description: Since this is a dbt model, the scope should be (dbt) not something else

assertions.name: Commit cleanup guidance provided
assertions.description: Should provide specific guidance on squashing or rebasing the messy commits (wip, fix tests, etc.)

assertions.name: #data-review message generated
assertions.description: Should generate a Slack message for #data-review channel with [REVIEW] prefix, ticket ID, and complexity/time estimate

assertions.name: PR description includes summary
assertions.description: PR description should have a summary section explaining what the PR does

evals.name: clean-commits-ready
evals.prompt: I need to create a PR for my work. I'm on branch DA-8823/fix-booking-metrics and I've already cleaned up my commits - I have 3 commits total: 'feat(analytics): update booking metrics calculation', 'test(analytics): add tests for new metrics', and 'docs(analytics): update README with metric definitions'. This fixes an issue where we were double-counting some bookings in our dashboards.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize commits are already clean, validate they follow format, generate PR title '(DA-8823) fix(analytics): correct booking metrics calculation', create PR, and generate review message

assertions.name: PR title matches regex format
assertions.description: The generated PR title must match the required format

assertions.name: PR title includes correct ticket ID
assertions.description: The PR title should include (DA-8823) for Data Analyst ticket

assertions.name: PR title uses 'fix' type
assertions.description: Since this fixes a double-counting bug, type should be 'fix' not 'feat'

assertions.name: PR title uses 'analytics' scope
assertions.description: The scope should be (analytics) matching the commit messages

assertions.name: Recognizes commits are clean
assertions.description: Should acknowledge that commits are already well-formatted and not suggest unnecessary changes

assertions.name: #data-review message generated
assertions.description: Should generate a Slack message for #data-review channel

assertions.name: PR description mentions the bug fix
assertions.description: Description should mention fixing the double-counting issue

evals.name: urgent-hotfix
evals.prompt: ok this is kinda urgent - found a bug in production in our recommendation model. I'm on a branch HF-9182/fix-recommendation-scoring and I have one commit '(HF-9182) fix(ml_jobs): correct feature normalization in ranking'. Need to get this PR up ASAP so someone can review and we can deploy. The bug was causing some items to get artificially boosted scores.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize urgency (HF prefix), validate commit format is good, create PR with appropriate urgency noted, generate review message with higher complexity/time estimate

assertions.name: PR title matches regex format
assertions.description: The generated PR title must match the required format

assertions.name: PR title includes HF ticket ID
assertions.description: The PR title should include (HF-9182) for HotFix

assertions.name: PR title uses 'fix' type
assertions.description: Type should be 'fix' for bug fix

assertions.name: PR title uses 'ml_jobs' scope
assertions.description: The scope should be (ml_jobs) for machine learning jobs

assertions.name: Urgency acknowledged
assertions.description: Should acknowledge the urgent nature and expedite the process

assertions.name: #data-review message indicates urgency
assertions.description: The Slack message should communicate this is urgent/high priority

assertions.name: PR description mentions production bug
assertions.description: Description should clearly state this fixes a production issue
